name: Rio Negro (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the sky over Patagonia and the rivers Negro and Limay
the snow of the Andes; peace
the irrigated valleys and forests of the province
wisdom and the Mapuche people, original inhabitants of the province

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — blue, white, green — with a black rectangular canton in the upper hoist (one-third of the flag's length and one-third of its height) bearing a circle of thirteen white five-pointed stars.
history: Adopted by provincial law 4350 on 8 October 2009 after a public competition. The flag was designed by Antonio Hernandez. Its tricolour evokes the Patagonian landscape — sky, snow and valley — while the black canton with thirteen white stars honours the Mapuche heritage of the province and represents the thirteen departments of Rio Negro.

features.symbolism: blue for the Patagonian sky and the rivers Negro and Limay; white for the snow of the Andes and peace; green for the fertile valleys and forests of the province
the canton represents the wisdom of the Mapuche people, original inhabitants of Rio Negro
the thirteen departments of the Province of Rio Negro arranged around the lucero (the morning star, Guñelve in Mapudungun)
